Article Title: The Aleph and the endgame: Lionel Messi's redemption meets Spain's tiki-taka in a World Cup final for ages
Article Text:
Lionel Messi

In celebrated Argentine writer Jorge Luis Borges’ short story “El Aleph”, the eponymous character is a mystical point in space that contains all other points in the universe, allowing whoever looks into it to see every place on Earth from every angle simultaneously without distortion.According to the giant of Latin American literature who died on June 14 in 1986 — eight days before Maradona’s two mythical goals against England at the World Cup in Mexico that Argentina would go on to win — a man is both the victim and the flow of his destiny.This Sunday, Lionel Messi — the Aleph in Argentina’s football atmosphere — will seek the infinite in the finite at the very place in New Jersey where, lonely and defeated a decade ago, he had decided to quit the game. The flow of his destiny has taken him thus far and it has drawn us in because of its rhapsodical and redemptive surface.Yet, Spain will be there, seeking their own moment of truth in this dreamspace and placing Messi’s journey in two parallels in this World Cup final. On the one hand, here’s a country which has put a neat bow on the process of making Messi complete; on the other hand there’s a now challenger who will be more than eager to cook the very performer’s goose in an engaging immersion into the reverie in which we might just see ourselves.If this continues to be a painful point of ambiguity in the narrative, it also becomes the contest’s central driving force because it always transports us into a new possibility.This is a final that speaks Spanish but it’s more than a contest, brimming with a banquet of emotional turbulence and enchantment while allowing so many subplots and such a stirring confluence of ideas layering upon each other.Never in the World Cup’s history had the reigning Copa América winners come to take on European champions in a final. The ‘Finalissima’ between the two continental kings was cancelled in March because of the war in the Gulf. But it’s happening now on the grandest stage and with the biggest stakes.This contest is also unraveling with a number of possibilities.Spain’s pass-heavy tiki-taka with all its fluidity and fantasy which had consumed France’s ambitions in the semifinal, will now meet its match in La Scaloneta’s counter-pressing and never-say-die attitude. This also holds attention with another convenient narrative taking stock of their past and present. Spain’s colectivismo — “Simply being ourselves as a team,” as De la Fuente said — is now up against Argentina’s play-for-Messi joie de vivre.Spain are unstoppable having remained unbeaten in normal or extra time for 37 matches. Argentina, themselves on an unbeaten run of 14 matches, provide them the ultimate test. It remains to be seen how Messi and his entourage make their trip against Marc Cucurella, Fabian Ruiz and Rodri into a complex odyssey across the landscape of two teams’ aspirations.Football is often played with a degree of mysticism which also makes it fold back on itself. Back in 2017 when Lionel Scaloni was at the Spanish football association’s coaching academy, he found in one Luis de la Fuente a teacher from whom he was more than happy to learn the craft.The two coaches, standing on the touchline and guiding their teams to immortality, may now be tempted to reflect on their shared past.
Article Summary: Lionel messi will seek the infinite in the finite at the very place in new jersey where, lonely and defeated a decade ago, he had decided to quit the game. The flow of his destiny has taken him thus far and it has drawn us in because of its rhapsodical and redemptive surface.

Article Title: WWE Saturday Night’s Main Event Results & Highlights (July 18, 2026): Roman Reigns & Jalen Brunson take out Seth Rollins, New champions and more
Article Text:
Roman Reigns and Jalen Brunson. Image via: WWE/ X

Paige & Brie Bella vs. Lainey Reed & Fallon Henley

Danhausen vs. JD McDonagh

Bayley vs. Lyra Valkyria

Cody Rhodes & CM Punk vs. Gunther & Sami Zayn

What happened in the final segment of WWE Saturday Night’s Main Event (July 18, 2026)?

WWE presented the July edition of Saturday Night’s Main Event from Madison Square Garden in New York City. The vintage show was a captivating event, continuing the road to SummerSlam 2026, and featured some exciting in-ring matches. The show closed out with an appearance by the World Heavyweight Champion, Roman Reigns , and his invitee, Jalen Brunson, who took down Seth Rollins , who had brought an ally of his own.The opening match of the event saw Brie Bella and Paige defend the WWE Women’s Tag Team titles against Fatal Influence’s Fallon Henley and Lainey Reed. The back-and-forth tag team bout set the tone for the event and was a good match. In the closing moments, Paige utilized a roll-up on Reed, but Jacy Jayne pushed her over, and Reed got the tights to score the pinfall and win the tag team titles for her team.This was a No Disqualification match. Dominik Mysterio interfered multiple times on behalf of his Judgment Day cohort, JD McDonagh. However, Danhausen brought backup of his own in the form of NBA star Karl Anthony Towns, who planted McDonagh and Mysterio with chokeslams, with Dirty Dom thrown on a table. Following that, Danhausen hit the reverse DDT on McDonagh to seal the victory.Lyra Valkyria debuted a new theme song to suit her new heel persona as she went one-on-one against her former friend Bayley.Early in the match, Valkyria’s nose was busted open, but she unleashed her fury on the Role Model. The bout concluded with Valkyria choking out Bayley and winning the match via submission.The final match of the night was a tag team bout with high-stakes implications. If Gunther and Sami Zayn won, they would be added to CM Punk and Cody Rhodes’ match at SummerSlam. Knowing the stakes, they worked well as a team, while Rhodes and Punk united to stop them. In the final moments, Gunther had Rhodes down for the three-count until SmackDown GM Nick Aldis returned after Gunther’s attack on him and stopped the referee. Aldis hit Gunther with the WWE title belt, allowing Punk to hit the GTS and placing Rhodes on him for the three count.Roman Reigns and Jalen Brunson came out to the ring. The OTC presented him with a customized New York Knicks World Heavyweight title. Seth Rollins then hit, but Tyrese Haliburton came out with a chair. Rollins came from behind to attack Reigns. However, he gained the upper hand and finished off Rollins with a spear. The show ended with Reigns holding the NBA MVP cup and Brunson holding Reigns’ World title.
Article Summary: Wwe presented the july edition of saturday night’s main event from madison square garden in new york city. The vintage show was a captivating event, continuing the road to summerslam 2026. The show closed out with an appearance by the world heavyweight champion, roman reigns, and his invitee, jalen brunson.

Article Title: NBA trade rumor: Warriors may add $50 million Mavericks star to the roster to reunite with Stephen Curry
Article Text:
Warriors and Ma​vericks trade rumor. Image via: Warriors and Mavericks​

Category Detail Player Klay Thompson Current Team Dallas Mavericks Rumored Team Golden State Warriors Contract Status Final year of a 3-year, $50 million deal signed in 2024 Salary Cap Hit Approximately $17.46 million (2026-27) Trade Likelihood Low unless the Warriors land LeBron James Latest Insider Update Jake Fischer reports mutual openness to a reunion Potential Return A buyout is considered more realistic than a trade, per reports

Which teams are interested in Klay Thompson?

Klay Thompson. Image via: Petre Thomas-Imagn Images

What are insiders saying about the trade rumor?

Contract details and salary cap implications

How the trade could impact both teams

Klay Thompson's expiring Mavericks contract has turned a throwaway autograph line into the NBA's newest reunion storyline. Dallas has shown no interest in a buyout, meaning any exit runs through a trade, but that hasn't stopped Golden State from reemerging as the sentimental frontrunner. The timing matters because the Warriors are also chasing LeBron James this offseason, and insiders now link both pursuits together.Golden State isn't the only team circling Thompson if Dallas moves on. The Miami Heat are reportedly interested contingent on a buyout, since Tim Hardaway Jr. is currently their only reliable outside shooter. The Los Angeles Lakers carry a family tie through Thompson's father, Mychal, a longtime figure in that organization. Detroit and Orlando have also been floated as fits, given both teams lack shooting depth. None of that momentum matches the emotional pull of Golden State, though, especially after Thompson told a fan who said they missed him on the Warriors, "Never say never."Jake Fischer reports Thompson and the Warriors would both welcome a reunion, particularly if Golden State wins the LeBron James sweepstakes and needs shooting around him."He's had interest in playing with LeBron in the past. I do think that there would be excitement from both sides, of Golden State and Klay, to really go out on a high note," Fischer said on his podcast.Analyst Charles North went further, arguing a Thompson return alongside James would recreate a lineup capable of rivaling any trio in league history when paired with Stephen Curry and Draymond Green. “Thompson returning to the Golden State Warriors would be absolutely amazing, especially if LeBron James went there as well. The starting five would be filled with legendary players whom fans would rush to their TV screens to watch each night. The fit is obviously great, with the chemistry between him, Steph Curry, and Draymond Green rivaling any other trio to ever play,” Charles North shared Not everyone is convinced a move alone fixes Golden State's roster problems given how the offseason has gone so far.Thompson is playing out the final season of the three-year, $50 million deal he signed with Dallas in 2024, carrying a base salary near $17.46 million for 2026-27. That expiring number makes him a manageable trade chip, though teams remain wary of taking on a 36-year-old whose production has dipped to 11.7 points per game on 38.3 percent three-point shooting last season. A buyout, rather than a trade, gives Golden State the clearer path since it would let Thompson sign for less than his current cap hit.For Dallas, moving on from Thompson closes the book on a pairing that never got going after Luka Doncic's trade reshaped the roster around him.For Golden State, adding Thompson gives Curry a familiar co-star and instant floor spacing, but only solves part of a roster that has otherwise had a quiet offseason. If James also signs, the Warriors could field a lineup built on institutional memory rather than fresh additions, a gamble that hinges entirely on decisions still pending in Dallas and free agency.The next checkpoint is Dallas's buyout market decision, expected before training camp opens. Thompson's next move now depends on choices made in two front offices at once.
Article Summary: Klay thompson's expiring contract has turned a throwaway autograph line into the nba's newest reunion storyline. The miami heat are reportedly interested contingent on a buyout, since tim hardaway jr. is currently their only reliable outside shooter. Detroit and orlando have also been floated as fits, given both teams lack shooting depth.

Article Title: France vs England FIFA World Cup third-place match result: Kylian Mbappe breaks all-time WC goal record as Bukayo Saka hat-trick fails France in 10-goal thriller
Article Text:
England's Jude Bellingham (10) shoots the ball past France goalkeeper Mike Maignan (16) to score their sixth goal during the World Cup third-place playoff match between France and England. (AP Photo)

A historic evening for French talisman Kylian Mbappé ended in bittersweet heartbreak as England clinched a wild 6-4 victory in an astonishing, high-scoring third-place playoff at the Hard Rock Stadium on Saturday night. While Mbappé cemented his status as a footballing immortal by becoming the all-time top scorer in World Cup history with 22 goals, it was a sensational hat-trick from Bukayo Saka and a late Jude Bellingham solo masterclass that earned England the bronze medal.Despite superstars Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham initially starting on the bench, a clinical England side tore through the French defense early on to establish a commanding 4-0 lead. France, still hurting from their semifinal exit against Spain, looked completely buried before their captain single-handedly dragged them back into contention.Mbappé began the rescue act in the 48th minute, smartly flicking a shot past Three Lions goalkeeper Dean Henderson. In the 66th minute, the 27-year-old superstar generated a moment of history. Meeting the ball inside the box, he unleashed a fierce left-footed strike from 14 yards to beat Henderson again, moving his tournament tally to 10 goals—two clear of Lionel Messi in the Golden Boot race.More importantly, the goal was his 22nd career World Cup strike, officially surpassing Messi's previous all-time record of 21 goals.Mbappé's second goal brought Les Bleus roaring back to a tight 4-3 deficit. However, England quickly reasserted their dominance. An unstoppable Bukayo Saka completed a brilliant hat-trick to keep the Three Lions ahead in the chaotic second half.France kept pushing, closing the gap to 5-4 through the goal of Dembele and threatening a dramatic equalizer. But Jude Bellingham, introduced as a late substitute, put the final exclamation mark on the match in added minutes. The Real Madrid midfielder produced a spectacular solo run, weaving past multiple French defenders before slotting the ball home to seal the incredible 6-4 victory. While England celebrated a thrilling finish to their campaign, Mbappé walked away with a historic milestone wrapped in defeat.
Article Summary: England beat france 6-4 in world cup third-place playoff at hard rock stadium. Kylian mbappé scored twice to become all-time top scorer in tournament history. Bukayo saka scored a hat-trick to keep england ahead in the second half.

Article Title: 'Perfect ending': FIFA president calls Messi vs Yamal World Cup final 'blessing from football gods'
Article Text:
FIFA president Gianni Infantino is seen prior to the World Cup semifinal soccer match between France and Spain in Arlington, Texas, near Dallas, Tuesday, July 14, 2026. (AP Photo/Eric Gay)

"A blessing from the football gods"

"This final is a blessing from the football gods,"

"We have Messi, Lamine Yamal, and many other exceptional players. It's incredible."

Argentina's Lionel Messi celebrates with his teammates at the end of the World Cup semifinal soccer match between England and Argentina in Atlanta, Wednesday, July 15, 2026. (AP Photo/Rebecca Blackwell)

"They are the two best teams. They deserved to be in this final and they have played exceptionally well."

"It wasn't easy to get here. There have been 48 teams, incredible matches, and a very challenging World Cup."

Messi and Yamal headline football's biggest stage

Spain's Lamine Yamal (19) celebrates at the end of the World Cup quarterfinal soccer match between Spain and Belgium in Inglewood, Calif., near Los Angeles, Friday, July 10, 2026. (AP Photo/Gregory Bull)

"There's Messi, there's Lamine Yamal, and there are many other exceptional footballers. But there's also that photo. It's incredible."

20-year-old soccer star Lionel Messi helps to bathe Lamine Yamal, who was merely six months old at the time, during a photo session in Sept. 2007 in the dressing room of the Camp Nou stadium in Barcelona, Spain. (AP Photo/Joan Monfort)

"The world is going to stop"





"It's going to be like fireworks. It's going to be incredible. The world is going to stop."

"Three billion people are going to watch how this World Cup ends."

"I imagined it would be big, but not like this. Not on this scale, with this atmosphere, this celebration, and this energy."

"There's a feeling of happiness all over the world. Of course, when you lose you're sad, but people have come from everywhere to celebrate football."

Why Infantino believes this has been the greatest World Cup





"The best players, the best teams, the people, the stadiums are packed, and it's a total celebration."

"The world stops and watches. It's the best in history, past and present, and it also prepares us for the future."

"We haven't had any incidents. People have come from all over the world, and everyone is celebrating football."

"All the champions have played, but also new teams and new countries. We've seen stories that only football can write and turn into legend."

Tournament has not been without controversy

President Donald Trump speaks as FIFA President Gianni Infantino listens at a reception at Trump Tower in New York, Friday, July 17, 2026. (AP Photo/Jacquelyn Martin)

FIFA president Gianni Infantino has described Sunday's FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina as "a blessing from the football gods", calling it the perfect ending to what he believes has been the greatest World Cup in history.Speaking in an exclusive interview with MARCA on the eve of the final at New York New Jersey Stadium, Infantino said football could not have wished for a bigger occasion, with reigning world champions Argentina led by Lionel Messi taking on a Spain side spearheaded by teenage sensation Lamine Yamal.For Infantino, the fixture represents far more than a battle for the trophy. It is, in his view, the meeting of football's greatest icon with the player many believe could define the sport's next generation.Infantino made no attempt to hide his excitement as he looked ahead to Sunday's showpiece.Infantino told MARCA.He pointed to the unique storyline surrounding Messi and Yamal as one of the defining images of the tournament.The FIFA president believes the finalists have fully earned their places after negotiating what he described as an exceptionally demanding tournament.Infantino added, Reflecting on the expanded 48-team competition.While acknowledging the quality spread across both squads, Infantino repeatedly returned to the symbolic image of Messi facing Yamal.The Argentina captain, now 39, is preparing for what could be the final World Cup match of his extraordinary career. Across the halfway line will stand 19-year-old Yamal, widely regarded as one of football's brightest young stars.For Infantino, that contrast perfectly captures the occasion.Believed to be referencing to a now-viral photograph from 2007 showing a 20-year-old Lionel Messi bathing a five-month-old Lamine Yamal in a plastic tub at Camp Nou. The moment was captured during a charity calendar photoshoot organised by FC Barcelona and UNICEF, and resurfaced in 2024, gaining massive global attention as Yamal rose to prominence.He believes the matchup transcends football itself, bringing together two generations in a single defining moment.Infantino predicted Sunday's final would attract an enormous global audience.He added.The FIFA president also praised the atmosphere throughout the tournament, saying the scale of the event had exceeded even his own expectations.According to Infantino, supporters from around the world have transformed the competition into a global celebration.Infantino did not hesitate when asked how he would rank the tournament."The World Cup is absolutely spectacular, unique, and incredible."He continued:For the FIFA president, the tournament has delivered everything the governing body hoped for, while also laying the foundations for future editions.He also highlighted what he described as the peaceful atmosphere created throughout the month-long tournament.Infantino concluded by praising both traditional football powers and emerging nations for contributing to the spectacle.Before ending the interview, he repeated his assessment in unmistakable terms."It's the best World Cup in history."Despite Infantino's glowing assessment, the 2026 World Cup has also been accompanied by several high-profile controversies.FIFA has faced criticism over a number of refereeing decisions during the tournament, particularly in matches involving defending champions Argentina. Disputed VAR calls and officiating decisions prompted the viral social media term "VARgentina", although FIFA's refereeing department and Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni have consistently rejected suggestions of favouritism and defended the integrity of the competition.Infantino has also faced scrutiny over FIFA's relationship with United States President Donald Trump after the governing body overturned the suspension of United States forward Folarin Balogun following Trump's public intervention. Human rights organisation FairSquare subsequently lodged a complaint with the International Olympic Committee's Ethics Commission, alleging breaches of political neutrality, while UEFA and the Belgian Football Association were among those critical of the decision.Separately, FIFA is also assessing disciplinary reports following Argentina players' post-match display of a banner reading "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" after their semifinal victory over England. FIFA has confirmed that its independent Disciplinary Committee is reviewing the relevant match reports before deciding whether any further action is warranted under the FIFA Disciplinary Code.None of those issues, however, have altered Infantino's view of the tournament.The World Cup final between Argentina and Spain will take place at the New York New Jersey Stadium (MetLife Stadium) in East Rutherford, New Jersey, kicking off at 12:30 AM IST on Monday, July 20, 2026. Infantino remains convinced that the competition has delivered the perfect ending, with the sport's greatest champion sharing the stage one last time with the player many believe could define the future of the tournament.
Article Summary: Fifa president gianni infantino says sunday's world cup final between spain and argentina is "a blessing from the football gods" he believes it has been the greatest world cup in history. Three billion people are expected to watch the final at new york new jersey stadium.

Article Title: Bukayo Saka joins exclusive England World Cup hat-trick club after breaking 60-year record
Article Text:
England's Bukayo Saka (7) celebrates scoring his side's 5th goal from the penalty spot during the World Cup third-place playoff soccer match between France and England in Miami Gardens, Fla., Saturday, July 18, 2026. (AP Photo/Lynne Sladky)





England race into commanding lead

England's Declan Rice, right, congrats Bukayo Saka (7) after scoring his third goal of the game from the penalty spot during the World Cup third-place playoff soccer match between France and England in Miami Gardens, Fla., Saturday, July 18, 2026. (AP Photo/Lynne Sladky)

France threaten comeback before Saka seals history

England's Bukayo Saka (7) is congratulated by Jude Bellingham (10) and Reece James (24) after scoring his side's 5th goal from the penalty spot during the World Cup third-place playoff soccer match between France and England in Miami Gardens, Fla., Saturday, July 18, 2026. (AP Photo/Lynne Sladky)



A hat-trick that rewrites England history





Sir Geoff Hurst (1966 World Cup Final vs West Germany)

Gary Lineker (1986 group stage vs Poland)

Harry Kane (2018 group stage vs Panama)

Bukayo Saka (2026 third-place playoff vs France)



Crowning one of the best seasons of his career

England's Bukayo Saka (7) celebrates scoring his side's fourth goal during the World Cup third-place playoff soccer match between France and England in Miami Gardens, Fla., Saturday, July 18, 2026. (AP Photo/Rebecca Blackwell)

England's Harry Kane (9) gestures alongside teammates, from left, Reece James (24), Djed Spence (25), Anthony Gordon (18), Bukayo Saka (7), Declan Rice (4) and Ezri Konsa (2) during the World Cup third-place playoff soccer match between France and England in Miami Gardens, Fla., Saturday, July 18, 2026. (AP Photo/Rebecca Blackwell)

Bukayo Saka produced the finest performance of his international career as England defeated France 6-4 in an extraordinary FIFA World Cup 2026 third-place playoff, becoming just the fourth English player ever to score a hat-trick at a World Cup and the first to do so in the tournament's knockout phase since Sir Geoff Hurst's iconic feat 60 years ago.Despite early fitness doubts that saw Noni Madueke start ahead of him earlier in the tournament, Saka ultimately emerged as England’s most efficient attacking weapon, finishing the World Cup with three goals and three assists in just 361 minutes. His impact in Miami underlined what many fans had been arguing throughout the knockout rounds.The Arsenal winger scored twice before halftime before completing his treble from the penalty spot late in the second half, inspiring England to their highest World Cup finish since lifting the trophy in 1966 and ensuring Thomas Tuchel's side ended the tournament with the bronze medal.For Saka, it was a performance that placed him alongside some of the greatest names in English football history.England made the perfect start in Miami. Declan Rice opened the scoring inside three minutes before Ezri Konsa doubled the advantage in the 18th minute as France struggled to cope with England's intensity.Saka then took centre stage, his first goal arrived in the 37th minute after combining neatly with Marcus Rashford.Although France goalkeeper Mike Maignan initially kept out his first effort, Saka reacted quickest to convert the rebound and make it 3-0.England were not finished before the break.Deep into first-half stoppage time, Konsa turned provider after driving forward from defence and picking out Saka, who finished clinically to give England a remarkable 4-0 halftime lead.It was an almost flawless opening 45 minutes for Tuchel's side, who appeared to have the contest wrapped up before the interval.France responded immediately after halftime. Kylian Mbappé pulled one back in the 48th minute before Bradley Barcola added another six minutes later to reduce England's lead to 4-2.Mbappé struck again in the 66th minute, bringing France within one goal at 4-3 and setting up a nervy finish after what had once looked like a one-sided contest.England eventually regained breathing space late in the match.After Malo Gusto fouled Djed Spence inside the penalty area in the closing stages, Jude Bellingham initially looked set to take the spot-kick but generously handed the responsibility to Saka, recognising the opportunity for his teammate to complete a hat-trick. Saka stepped forward in the 87th minute and calmly sent the goalkeeper the wrong way from the spot to seal his treble and restore England's two-goal advantage.Although Ousmane Dembélé scored deep into stoppage time to make it 5-4, substitute Jude Bellingham rounded off the scoring moments later as England secured a dramatic 6-4 victory.The performance only intensified debate around England’s semifinal defeat to Argentina, where a fully fit Saka was left as an unused substitute in the 2-1 loss. Thomas Tuchel later admitted he “just had a feeling” about using different tactical options, a decision that drew widespread criticism from supporters.Saka's treble secured his place in one of the most exclusive groups in English football.He became only the fourth England men's player ever to score a hat-trick at a FIFA World Cup, joining:While Lineker and Kane achieved their hat-tricks during the group stage, Saka's came during the knockout phase of the tournament.That distinction puts him alongside only one other England player.Sir Geoff Hurst famously scored the only hat-trick ever recorded in a men's World Cup final when England defeated West Germany 4-2 after extra time at Wembley in 1966.For six decades, no England player had managed another World Cup knockout hat-trick.Now, exactly 60 years later, Saka has matched one of the country's most celebrated World Cup records.The performance also capped an outstanding campaign for both club and country.Saka has now contributed 14 goals and 12 assists across all competitions this season, helping Arsenal win their first league title in 22 years before becoming England's standout attacking player at the World Cup.His hat-trick also took his England tally to 17 international goals and six goals at the 2026 World Cup, reinforcing his status as one of the most decisive players in the tournament despite limited minutes.Saka himself later admitted he had been eager to play a bigger role earlier in the knockout stages, leaving a lingering “what if” over England’s campaign, particularly given his decisive impact in the third-place playoff.Perhaps just as significantly, England finally ended a long wait for a podium finish at football's biggest tournament.After losing the third-place playoff to Italy in 1990 and Belgium in 2018 following semifinal exits, the Three Lions avoided another disappointment to claim the bronze medal, securing their best World Cup finish since 1966.The match itself will be remembered as one of the greatest third-place playoffs in World Cup history, producing 10 goals and several dramatic momentum swings.But above everything else, it belonged to Bukayo Saka.Sixty years after Sir Geoff Hurst etched his name into England folklore at Wembley, another England forward has now joined that exclusive company, producing a World Cup hat-trick that will stand among the finest individual performances in the nation's football history.
Article Summary: England defeated france 6-4 in an extraordinary fifa world cup 2026 third-place playoff. Bukayo saka became just the fourth english player ever to score a hat-trick at a world cup and the first to do so in the tournament's knockout phase since sir geoff hurst's iconic feat 60 years ago.

Article Title: Declan Rice enters World Cup history books after breaking 90-year-old record with first World Cup goal
Article Text:
England's Declan Rice (4) celebrates scoring their first goal during the World Cup third-place playoff soccer match between France and England in Miami Gardens, Fla., Saturday, July 18, 2026. (AP Photo/Rebecca Blackwell)

Fastest goals in third-place playoff history

Hakan Şükür (Turkey) – 11 seconds vs South Korea (2002) Ernst Lehner (Germany) – 25 seconds vs Austria (1934) Declan Rice (England) – 2 minutes 14 seconds vs France (2026)

England's earliest major tournament goal in years

England's Declan Rice (4) scores their first goal as France's Malo Gusto (2) and Desire Doue (20) defend during the World Cup third-place playoff soccer match between France and England in Miami Gardens, Fla., Saturday, July 18, 2026. (AP Photo/Rebecca Blackwell)



A complete midfield performance

Joining another exclusive England club





Roger Hunt (vs Mexico, 1966)

Joe Cole (vs Sweden, 2006)

Raheem Sterling (vs Iran, 2022)

Jude Bellingham (vs Panama, 2026)

Harry Kane (vs Mexico, 2026)

Declan Rice (vs France, 2026)

Arsenal history and England's best finish in decades

Declan Rice etched his name into both England and FIFA World Cup history after scoring the fastest goal of his international career during England's remarkable 6-4 victory over France in the 2026 World Cup third-place playoff.The England captain struck after just 2 minutes and 14 seconds in Miami to score his first-ever World Cup goal, setting the tone for one of the most extraordinary matches in tournament history. The early strike helped launch England towards their highest World Cup finish since 1966 while also earning Rice a place among several exclusive historical lists.His goal was not just important because it opened the scoring. It also became one of the quickest ever recorded in a World Cup third-place playoff.According to statistics compiled by MisterChip, Rice's opener is the third-fastest goal ever scored in a FIFA World Cup third-place match.Only two players have found the net more quickly:?Şükür's strike remains the fastest goal in FIFA World Cup history across every stage of the tournament.More than 90 years after Lehner's effort in Italy, Rice has now joined that exclusive list with England's earliest breakthrough in a World Cup bronze medal match.Rice's strike also represented England's fastest goal in a competitive international fixture since Luke Shaw scored after 1 minute and 57 seconds against Italy in the UEFA Euro 2020 final.Wearing the captain's armband, Rice collected possession outside the penalty area before driving a precise low finish beyond Mike Maignan to hand England the perfect start.It was also the midfielder's first World Cup goal after previously playing supporting roles during England's recent tournament campaigns.Rice's influence did not end with the opener. Fifteen minutes later, he delivered a superb corner that Ezri Konsa headed into the net to double England's advantage.That assist earned Rice another notable milestone.According to Opta, he became the first England player since David Beckham at the 2002 World Cup to register two assists directly from corner kicks during a single World Cup tournament.Rice's first corner assist of the competition came in England's 4-2 group-stage victory over Croatia, where he provided Harry Kane with a headed goal from a set piece. Beckham achieved the same feat in Korea and Japan by creating goals for Sol Campbell against Sweden in the group stage and Rio Ferdinand against Denmark in the round of 16. Twenty-four years later, Rice has matched that accomplishment.Rice also became only the sixth England player to both score and provide an assist in the same FIFA World Cup match.The exclusive list now includes:His contribution proved decisive as England surged into a 4-0 halftime lead before eventually overcoming a spirited French comeback to win 6-4.Rice also helped Arsenal achieve another unusual piece of World Cup history.Alongside Bukayo Saka, who scored a hat-trick against France, the pair became only the second Arsenal duo ever to score in the same men's World Cup match.The first were Netherlands teammates Marc Overmars and Dennis Bergkamp, who both scored against South Korea during the 1998 World Cup group stage in Marseille shortly after helping Arsenal win the domestic Double.For Rice, however, the individual records mattered less than the team achievement.His first World Cup goal helped England secure the bronze medal after a thrilling 10-goal contest, ending the tournament on a high following semifinal heartbreak against Argentina.In the space of one unforgettable afternoon, Rice not only celebrated his first goal on football's biggest stage but also joined several of England's most distinguished company, producing a performance that will stand among the finest of his international career.
Article Summary: Declan rice scored the fastest goal of his international career during england's remarkable 6-4 victory over france in the 2026 world cup third-place playoff. The england captain struck after just 2 minutes and 14 seconds in miami to score his first-ever world cup goal.

Article Title: Beyond Tiki-Taka: The tactical evolution that has Spain one win from FIFA World Cup glory
Article Text:
Image credit: PTI

Beyond tiki-taka

Spain didn't stop France. They annihilated them

A defence that's gone under the radar

Lamine Yamal, playing a different game for his country

Collective understanding over individual moments

The final challenge: Stopping Messi

A month ago, when the World Cup got underway, Spain were among the favourites, though barely anyone was talking about them. Most of the pre-tournament chatter centred on France's frontline, Argentina defending their title, and whether this might be the last World Cup for Lionel Messi or Cristiano Ronaldo Spain just got on with it. A goalless draw against Cape Verde in the opener did nothing to change that narrative. Since then, though, this has been one of the most complete tactical campaigns of the tournament: top of Group H, a single goal conceded across the knockout rounds, and a 2-0 dismantling of France in the semi-final that leaves them one win away from a second World Cup title.For the best part of two decades, Spain have meant one thing: tiki-taka. Endless possession, short passing triangles, patience bordering on suffocation. It was the defining feature of the greatest generation this country has produced.This side plays differently. Luis de la Fuente hasn't abandoned the old principles of control through the ball, but he's cut away the predictability that came with them. There's more verticality now, more aggression, more urgency to the passing.It isn't a new development so much as a return, and the numbers trace it clearly.At the 2018 World Cup, where Spain went out on penalties in the last 16, their share of progressive passes sat at 0.82 times the tournament average. In 2022, another round-of-16 exit on penalties, it had dropped further, to 0.76. Then the turn: at Euro 2024, which Spain won, that figure rose to 1.08 times the field average. Now, in this World Cup final, it sits at 1.09. Both exits came in below the average. Both the European champions and the World Cup finalists have sat above it.Oliver Kahn, the former Germany goalkeeper and Zee5 expert, thinks that shift explains most of what's happened this summer."They further developed tiki-taka in the last 10 or 12 years. Luis de la Fuente has made some adaptations. They are playing more vertically and much more aggressively, with both full-backs always attacking. This is a totally different Spain than it was 10 years ago."Robbie Fowler, the former England striker and fellow Zee5 expert, put it similarly in a media session."Everyone goes on about tiki-taka, and I don't think this is the tiki-taka of Spain that we've seen in the past. They are still a possession-based team, but what I love is it's possession in the right way, it's possession in the right spaces. There's a purpose to how they play."France came into Dallas with, arguably, the most frightening attack left in the competition. Kylian Mbappe led the race for the Golden Boot. Michael Olise had been one of the tournament's sharpest creators. Ousmane Dembele and Bradley Barcola had been repeatedly cutting teams open in transition all summer.Spain took every single one of those threats away.The scoreline against France told part of the story. It didn't come close to capturing how one-sided the game actually was. No semi-finalist had been reduced to as little as France were that night since Sweden, eight World Cups ago. France's expected goals per game had averaged 2.4 through the tournament; against Spain it fell to 0.31, their lowest figure of the summer. Spain allowed them 0.6 xG and generated 1.7 of their own, on a night that was built more around control than chance creation.France's whole approach through the tournament had relied on winning the ball back and going vertical immediately, exploiting the space behind defences with raw pace. So Spain simply refused to let that happen. The counter-press came instantly. The moment France won possession, Rodri, Fabian Ruiz and Dani Olmo were already closing the passing lanes before Les Bleus could get up any speed. Mbappe spent most of the evening chasing long balls that never quite arrived. Dembele was cut off from the game. Olise, usually France's creative pulse, had his quietest night of the tournament. Mbappe didn't register a single shot on target all match. France managed 10 attempts in total, despite 152 touches in the attacking third.Spain's attacking play tends to get the headlines. The back four deserve just as much attention, maybe more.Pedro Porro, Pau Cubarsi, Aymeric Laporte and Marc Cucurella have, hands down, become one of the strongest defensive units in the tournament. Across the knockout stage, Spain have conceded a combined 1.59 expected goals, which is a remarkable number for a team that has gone this deep.Against France specifically, Porro, Rodri, Laporte, Cubarsi and Ruiz won 25 of 34 individual duels and made 44 defensive actions between them. And they didn't break shape to do it.But perhaps the biggest reason behind Spain's success is the one few are talking about: Rodri.He's come back from a difficult club season, one shaped by the serious knee injury he suffered in September 2024, and found something close to his best form exactly when his country needed it. Against France, attacks kept running into him, whether he was intercepting a pass, mopping up loose possession, or simply slowing the game down when Spain needed a breath.The numbers back it up. Rodri has completed 655 passes at this World Cup, more than any player has managed in a single edition since records began in 1966. It's not just a possession stat; it says something about how much of Spain's rhythm runs through one man.Kahn frames it as a system built to work because the individuals inside it are exceptional."They have a perfect system, but you always need outstanding guys like Rodri and Lamine."Alongside Fabian Ruiz and Dani Olmo, Rodri simply outplayed Adrien Rabiot and Aurelien Tchouameni in midfield, and France never found a way back into that battle.Even when Didier Deschamps threw on Desire Doue, Manu Kone and Rayan Cherki looking for a way back into the game, Spain just adjusted their pressing triggers and stayed compact. Every substitution France made got met with a corresponding tactical answer from the Spanish bench.On paper, Yamal's tournament has been quiet. Five matches, one goal, no assists, a long way off the 24 goals and 17 assists he racked up for Barcelona this season.The numbers don't tell you much, though.His running alone forced Lucas Digne into the foul that gave Spain their penalty and their lead against France. Across the tournament he's stretched defences, driven the ball into deep areas, and put opponents on the back foot just by being on the pitch.Kahn thinks his role with Spain is simply different to the one he plays at club level."Lamine was coming back from an eight-week injury. He plays a little differently than at Barcelona. There he has much more freedom. Genius players like him and Messi need that freedom.""In the Spain team, he has to bring himself into the system. He has to play more for the team, not so much for himself."Even so, Kahn believes the teenager is built for exactly this kind of stage."He's only 19 years old. He has to earn that freedom. Now he has the chance to show in the final what a great player he is. I've got huge respect for a 19-year-old playing in a World Cup final."What stands out most, maybe, is that Spain no longer need one player to produce magic. Everyone on the pitch seems to know when to press, when to sit back into shape, when to break forward.Fowler sees it as possession with a purpose behind it, rather than possession as an end in itself."They still play possession-based football, but I think there's more directness. They can break the press with a simple pass through to Rodri or Olmo, and from a transitional point of view they're very, very quick."That balance between control and directness has made them one of the hardest teams in the world to actually play against.Standing in the way of a second title are the defending champions, Argentina, led by Messi, who at 39 is somehow playing better than he did in 2022. His expected goal rate has doubled, from 0.26 per 90 minutes four years ago to 0.52 now, and his shot volume and final-third receptions are up to match. Where Spain have built a system that no longer depends on any single player producing a moment of magic, Argentina's route through this tournament has leaned far more heavily on what one man can still do.For Fowler, stopping them starts with limiting what the eight-time Ballon d'Or winner can do on the ball."You've got to have players prepared to put in the hard yards, prepared to block tackles, block channels, run with the runners and don't allow Messi to get on the ball."Spain have already shown this tournament that they can strip elite attacking talent of its influence. They shut down Belgium. They reduced France's frontline to almost nothing. Now they face arguably the hardest job in football: stopping Messi from running a World Cup final.Kahn sees a story within the story here."The difference is one is 19, the other is 40. Both are coming from Barcelona. Both are coming from La Masia. The young guy plays against his role model. That's a great story."Messi chasing back-to-back titles, one last defining moment in a career that's already had plenty of them. Yamal, the teenager tipped to be the sport's next great one, walking into his first World Cup final. It's hard to script something better.Spain haven't lost in 37 straight internationals, matching Italy's world record run from 2018 to 2021, and go into the final having climbed to No. 1 in the FIFA rankings. There aren't as many household names in this squad as there were in 2010. Judged on performance rather than reputation, it might not need them. This may well be the most complete Spain team in over a decade.Argentina and Messi stand between them and a second star on the shirt.
Article Summary: Spain beat france 2-0 in the world cup semi-final in dallas. Luis de la fuente's side are one win away from a second world cup title. They are playing more vertically and much more aggressively than in the past. Rodri has completed 655 passes at this world cup, more than any player has managed in a single edition since records began in 1966.

Article Title: Jude Bellingham breaks all-time England goalscoring record with sensational strike in World Cup bronze thriller win against France
Article Text:
England's Jude Bellingham (10) celebrates scoring their sixth goal during the World Cup third-place playoff match between France and England.(AP Photo)

Jude Bellingham has firmly written his name into the record books of English football. In a chaotic and breathtaking 10-goal thriller against France during Saturday's World Cup third-place playoff, the 23-year-old midfielder produced a moment of absolute genius to seal a 6-4 victory for England and stand alone as the nation’s greatest ever single-tournament scorer.With his late sensational solo goal at the Hard Rock Stadium, Bellingham reached seven goals in this World Cup campaign. According to sports analytics Opta, those seven strikes are now the outright most ever scored by an England player at a single major international tournament, surpassing the legendary six-goal tallies of Gary Lineker (1986 World Cup) and Harry Kane (2018 World Cup and the ongoing edition of 2026).The historic milestone arrived in the dying minutes of a wild second half. France had mounted a furious comeback, driven by a record-breaking performance from Kylian Mbappé, pulling the scoreline back to a tight 5-4. With the match hanging in the balance during stoppage time, Bellingham took matters into his own hands.Receiving the ball near the halfway line, the Real Madrid powerhouse drove aggressively toward the French defense.Displaying a rare mix of raw power, agility, and elite technical skill, Bellingham skipped past three sliding French defenders, cutting sharply into the penalty box. Faced with an oncoming goalkeeper, he calmly slotted a low, composed finish into the bottom corner. The brilliant solo strike put the final exclamation mark on a memorable match, securing the bronze medal for the Three Lions.Bellingham’s path to this record is nothing short of extraordinary. After being dropped from the starting lineup in the immediate build-up to the World Cup by manager Thomas Tuchel, his spot was heavily questioned. However, the midfielder responded with massive tournament performances, including critical doubles against Mexico and Norway before his historic closing act against France.While Arsenal winger Bukayo Saka earned heavy praise for scoring a magnificent hat-trick earlier in the match, Bellingham’s record-breaking seventh goal stole the headlines. As England ends its campaign in the United States on a winning note, the young midfielder's historic goal scoring exploit sets a completely new benchmark for future generations of English talent.v
Article Summary: Jude bellingham scored seven goals in england's 6-4 win over france. The 23-year-old has now scored more goals than gary lineker and harry kane in a single major international tournament. Bellingham's goal came in the dying minutes of the world cup third-place playoff match.

What is at stake in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final?

The biggest prize in football is between two giants. World Cup 2026: Argentina vs Spain date, time, venue FIFA World Cup 2026 final between defending champions Argentina and European champions Spain will be played on July 19 at New York New Jersey Stadium. Lionel Messi is chasing another piece of history and Spain is looking to win its second World Cup so fans everywhere are ready for a final that should have great soccer, contrasting styles and a global audience.The World Cup final will kick off at, atHere are the key start times in major regions:The match will be officiated by, who takes charge of the tournament's biggest fixture.Argentina's road to the championship match has been anything but comfortable. Lionel Messi and his teammates repeatedly found ways to survive difficult knockout games. They needed extra time against Cape Verde in the Round of 32 and Switzerland in the quarterfinals. The defending champions also produced dramatic late comebacks against Egypt and England after trailing deep into the second half.Those narrow escapes underline Argentina's resilience. The squad has consistently responded under pressure and enters the final with confidence built on overcoming adversity.Spain, by contrast, has reached the final through control rather than chaos. The European champions opened the knockout stage with a convincing victory over Austria before edging Portugal and Belgium by a single goal. Their biggest statement came in the semifinal, where they comfortably defeated France while dominating possession for long stretches.The final therefore presents an intriguing tactical battle. Argentina has thrived in emotional, high-pressure contests, while Spain has relied on disciplined structure and patient football throughout the knockout rounds.Fans can watch the FIFA World Cup 2026 final through FIFA's official broadcast partners in their respective countries.Both finalists await history. Argentina has the opportunity to join two of the most exclusive clubs in football as the third country ever to successfully defend the FIFA World Cup title. Another victory would add to Lionel Messi’s already impressive international legacy.Spain, meanwhile, is a win away from claiming a second world title after years of rebuilding into one of the most powerful teams in Europe. They have played every knockout game with their cool, possession-based style and now face the ultimate test.The FIFA World Cup 2026 final is going to be one of the defining sporting moments of the year with the world’s biggest trophy, two champion teams and a packed stadium with millions watching around the world.

How Josh Kerr could earn money after retirement

Scottish middle-distance runner Josh Kerr has become one of the biggest names in athletics after winning global titles and consistently competing with the world’s best runners. His success on the track has basically boosted his commercial worth a lot, which leads to sponsorships and extra appearance opportunities. While Josh Kerr’s exact net worth has never been publicly disclosed, most industry estimates put it somewhere in the low millions of dollars for 2026. Fans have been kind of hunting around for his finances after yet another successful season, and also because his long term partnership with Brooks keeps growing. Nowadays, his income comes mostly from prize money, brand sponsorship deals, appearance fees, and small performance bonuses too.There still isn’t any official financial disclosure from Josh Kerr or from the people managing him, so his exact wealth is basically a mystery. Even so, some reports put his 2026 net worth somewhere in the low-million-dollar range, and they usually point to his success as a top-level middle-distance runner.Looking at his career, it includes Olympic and World Championship medals, regular Diamond League appearances, and a handful of record-breaking runs. All of that has likely translated into prize money from major events, while at the same time raising his “market value” with sponsors- that kind of indirect but real boost.One of the most meaningful boosts to his earnings is said to have come from Brooks. The sportswear company signed Kerr in 2018 and then renewed the partnership later on all the way through 2034.That long-term deal is believed to cover more than just standard sponsorship payments; it may also come with ambassador-type duties, so in practice it becomes one of the major income engines in his career.Then in 2026, Kerr reportedly received a $50,000 bonus after setting a new mile record in London. So you can see that performance incentives still stack on top of his yearly income, even after everything else.Josh Kerr makes money from several places, rather than depending only on race winnings. The biggest chunk of his income is thought to roll in from his steady Brooks sponsorship over the long run.Since he is one of the company’s leading athletes, Kerr gets salary payments, plus promotional campaigns, and ambassador work as well. When he does well in international events, he also picks up prize money from competitions and there are bonuses when he turns in exceptional performances.Then there are appearance fees from top track meetings, speaking events and promo shows, which is basically another stream. And as his global name keeps expanding, these commercial chances should only grow more, with time.Unlike many athletes who openly discuss investments, Josh Kerr has kept his financial portfolio private. There is no verified public information confirming investments in real estate, startups, or business ventures.For that reason, it would be inaccurate to credit him with business interests that have not been officially reported. At present, his wealth is primarily linked to his athletic career and endorsement agreements.Despite his growing success, Kerr maintains a relatively private lifestyle. His public appearances mostly revolve around competitions, training, and sponsor events.He regularly shares training updates with fans but keeps much of his personal life away from the spotlight.There is also no verified public information about an extensive luxury car collection or expensive watch collection. Any claims about such assets remain unconfirmed.Josh Kerr has established himself as one of Scotland’s greatest middle-distance runners. His Olympic medal, World Championship title, and record-breaking performances have earned him recognition among the sport’s elite. Beyond medals, his long-term Brooks partnership highlights his value as one of athletics’ most marketable stars.Even after retiring from competition, Kerr is expected to have several earning opportunities. Coaching, broadcasting, public speaking, brand ambassador roles, and continued partnerships with Brooks could help generate income.If he chooses to expand into business or investments in the future, those ventures could further increase his overall wealth. For now, however, his financial success remains closely tied to his achievements on the track and his long-term sponsorship agreements.
Article Summary: Scottish middle-distance runner josh kerr has become one of the biggest names in athletics after winning global titles and consistently competing with the world’s best runners. His income comes mostly from prize money, brand sponsorship deals, appearance fees, and small performance bonuses too.

The Atlanta Falcons have a star receiver in Drake London, while tight end Kyle Pitts and running back Bijan Robinson demand attention over the middle of the field. Boutte could thrive in that environment, with so many defensive eyes on Atlanta’s star names, allowing him plenty of one-on-one opportunities downfield. ”The New England Patriots’ star player Kayshon Boutte is all set to enter the final year of his deal with the team for the 2026 season.For the New England Patriots, trading Kayshon Boutte would clear $3.67 million in cap space off the team’s books.But the team would be hit with a dead cap hit of just $46,818.For the Washington Commanders, trading for Kayshon Boutte would immediately reunite him with his former LSU quarterback, Jayden Daniels, as he could be a cost effective, safer replacement target after their pursuit of Brandon Aiyuk fizzled out.For the Atlanta Falcons, trading for Kayshon Boutte is a bit expensive but he would be a proven WR3 option to take pressure off their primary targets
